meta.note: AI-generated tags — may be inaccurate. Contact page to report issues.

references.title: LearnCpp.com
references.description: The most comprehensive free C++ tutorial site. Covers everything from basics to advanced modern C++. Primary reading companion to Roberts.

references.title: CppReference
references.description: The authoritative C++ language and standard library reference. Not a tutorial — use this to look up exact behavior, function signatures, and standard guarantees.

references.title: Compiler Explorer (Godbolt)
references.description: Run C++ code in your browser and see the assembly output. Invaluable for understanding what the compiler actually does with your code.

references.title: CppQuiz
references.description: Daily C++ quiz questions covering tricky language behavior, undefined behavior, and subtle gotchas. Great for testing real understanding.

references.title: Exercism C++ Track
references.description: 100 C++ exercises with community mentoring. Covers all major concepts in order. The best structured exercise path available for free.

references.title: roadmap.sh — C++
references.description: Visual learning roadmap for C++. Good for seeing the big picture of what to learn and in what order.

references.title: NeetCode
references.description: Structured algorithm and data structure problems with video explanations. Best used after learning C++ basics. Great for interview prep.

references.title: Codecrafters
references.description: Build real tools from scratch — Redis, Git, HTTP server, shell — with guided tests. C++ supported. One of the best ways to level up after basics.

references.title: Codewars
references.description: Community-driven coding kata in C++ and many other languages. Good for extra practice and seeing other solutions.

references.title: CppCon YouTube Channel
references.description: Recorded talks from the annual C++ conference. Covers everything from beginner concepts to cutting-edge proposals. Watch after you have the basics.

references.title: W3Schools C++
references.description: Quick syntax reference and try-it editor. Good for fast lookups but lacks depth. Not a substitute for LearnCpp.

references.title: Operating Systems: Three Easy Pieces
references.description: The clearest, most practical OS textbook available free online. Essential for understanding what your C++ programs run on top of.

references.title: MIT 6.S081 Operating Systems
references.description: MIT's OS course with labs in C. Serious systems programming. Come here after OSTEP for hands-on kernel work.

references.title: The Linux Programming Interface
references.description: The definitive Linux API reference. Comprehensive coverage of system calls, POSIX, file I/O, processes, signals, and more.

references.title: MIT 6.046 — Design and Analysis of Algorithms
references.description: MIT's algorithms course with lecture videos, problem sets, and exams. Rigorous treatment of sorting, graphs, dynamic programming, and NP-completeness.

references.title: CS:APP — Computer Systems: A Programmer's Perspective
references.description: CMU's deep systems textbook. Covers everything from bits to caches to concurrency. One of the best CS books ever written.

references.title: OverTheWire: Bandit
references.description: Linux command line wargame for beginners. Learn bash, file permissions, processes, and basic security through puzzles.

references.title: LearnAnything.xyz
references.description: Graphical interface linking learning pathways across many CS and non-CS topics. Good for discovering what you don't know you should learn.

references.title: Free Programming Books (GitHub)
references.description: Massive curated list of free programming books in every language and topic. Good starting point when looking for free learning material.

references.title: System Design Primer
references.description: Comprehensive guide to system design concepts. Interview-focused but genuinely educational. Good when C++ fundamentals are solid.

references.title: Paul's Online Math Notes
references.description: Concise, clear math tutorials covering calculus, algebra, and differential equations. Great free resource when you need math for algorithms or graphics.

references.title: MIT Linear Algebra (Gilbert Strang)
references.description: Gilbert Strang's legendary linear algebra course. Free video lectures and problem sets. Essential for graphics, ML, and numerical computing.

references.title: Stanford CS140e
references.description: Practical OS course building a real OS in Rust for Raspberry Pi. Excellent systems intuition builder.

references.title: University of Helsinki — Programming MOOC
references.description: Free, self-paced programming course from the University of Helsinki — one of the most thorough beginner courses available. Covers programming fundamentals from the ground up with a heavy emphasis on problem-solving and clean code. Language-agnostic thinking that transfers directly to C++.

references.title: LunarVim
references.description: Community-driven Neovim configuration. Excellent IDE-like experience in the terminal for C++ development.

resources.title: Programming Abstractions in C++ — Eric Roberts
resources.description: The primary textbook this site follows. Exceptional pedagogy — starts from first principles and builds to graphs and inheritance. Buy this book.

resources.title: C++ Software Design — Klaus Iglberger
resources.description: Modern C++ design patterns and principles. Read after Roberts when you want to write production-quality code, not just working code.

resources.title: C++ Concurrency in Action — Anthony Williams
resources.description: The definitive book on multithreading in C++. Covers std::thread, atomics, memory ordering, and lock-free data structures.

resources.title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications — Martin Kleppmann
resources.description: The best book on distributed systems and data infrastructure. Not C++ specific but essential reading for systems engineers.

resources.title: Computer Systems: A Programmer's Perspective — Bryant & O'Hallaron
resources.description: Covers the full stack from bits to processes to networking. Deeply informs how to write efficient C++ by understanding what runs underneath.

resources.title: Elements of Programming Interviews — Adnan Aziz
resources.description: Interview preparation book with C++ solutions. Dense problem set covering arrays, strings, trees, graphs, and dynamic programming.

resources.title: Daily C++ Interview
resources.description: One C++ interview question per day format. Good for maintaining sharpness and discovering knowledge gaps.

resources.title: Introduction to Algorithms (CLRS)
resources.description: The canonical algorithms textbook. Dense and mathematical. Use alongside MIT 6.046 lectures. Not beginner-friendly but comprehensive.

resources.title: The Algorithm Design Manual — Skiena
resources.description: More practical than CLRS. War stories from real algorithm problems plus a catalog of known algorithms. Easier to read than CLRS.

resources.title: How to Prove It — Daniel Velleman
resources.description: Introduction to mathematical proofs and logic. Essential background for algorithm analysis and theoretical CS.

resources.title: Understanding Analysis — Abbott
resources.description: Rigorous real analysis text. Relevant when numerical methods or floating point precision matters in your C++ work.

resources.title: Crafting Interpreters — Robert Nystrom
resources.description: Build a complete interpreter from scratch in Java then C. The C implementation is excellent systems C++ adjacent work. Free online.

build_your_own.title: Build Your Own Redis from Scratch
build_your_own.description: Build a Redis-like key-value store in C++. Covers event loops, networking, and data structures.

build_your_own.title: Write a Shell in C
build_your_own.description: Build a Unix shell with forking, pipes, and job control. Direct C — translates cleanly to C++.

build_your_own.title: Build Your Own Shell (tutorial series)
build_your_own.description: Step-by-step shell implementation with tests. More structured than the Brennan tutorial.

build_your_own.title: Build Your Own Text Editor (kilo)
build_your_own.description: Build a terminal text editor in C under 1000 lines. Covers raw terminal mode, VT100 escape codes, and file I/O.

build_your_own.title: Designing a Simple Text Editor (C++)
build_your_own.description: C++ text editor using the FLTK GUI toolkit.

build_your_own.title: Operating Systems: From 0 to 1
build_your_own.description: Build a small OS from scratch in C. Covers bootloader, kernel, interrupts, and memory management.

build_your_own.title: Write Your Own Operating System (video series)
build_your_own.description: Video series building an OS in C++. Good complement to the C-based OS tutorials.

build_your_own.title: Malloc is not magic — Implementing Your Own Memory Allocator
build_your_own.description: Build malloc from scratch. Directly relevant to the arena-alloc project in the C++ Starter Pack.

build_your_own.title: Let's Build a Simple Database
build_your_own.description: Build a SQLite clone in C from scratch. Covers B-trees, paging, and SQL parsing.

build_your_own.title: Ray Tracing in One Weekend
build_your_own.description: Build a ray tracer from scratch in C++ over a weekend. Beautiful output, teaches vectors and graphics fundamentals.

build_your_own.title: Physically Based Rendering: From Theory to Implementation
build_your_own.description: The definitive book on photorealistic rendering in C++. Full source code available. Advanced but comprehensive.

build_your_own.title: Raycasting Engine of Wolfenstein 3D
build_your_own.description: Build the Wolfenstein 3D raycasting engine. Simpler than ray tracing, teaches the core 3D illusion technique.

build_your_own.title: How to Write an Emulator (CHIP-8 Interpreter)
build_your_own.description: Build a CHIP-8 emulator in C++. Great introduction to emulation — simple enough to finish in a weekend.

build_your_own.title: Emulation Tutorial — GameBoy Emulator
build_your_own.description: Full GameBoy emulator in C++. Much more complex than CHIP-8 — do that first.

build_your_own.title: NES Emulator From Scratch (video)
build_your_own.description: YouTube series building a full NES emulator in C++. Detailed and well-explained.

build_your_own.title: Let's Code a TCP/IP Stack
build_your_own.description: Build a TCP/IP network stack from scratch in C. Deep dive into networking fundamentals.

build_your_own.title: Beej's Guide to Network Programming
build_your_own.description: The classic free networking guide in C. Covers sockets, TCP, UDP. Essential systems programming reference.

build_your_own.title: Breakout (OpenGL)
build_your_own.description: Build Breakout in C++ with OpenGL. Teaches sprite rendering, collision detection, and game loop architecture.

build_your_own.title: Space Invaders from Scratch
build_your_own.description: Build Space Invaders in C++ from scratch with no engine. Part-by-part blog series.

build_your_own.title: Kaleidoscope: Implementing a Language with LLVM
build_your_own.description: Build a compiler for a simple language using LLVM in C++. The canonical LLVM tutorial.

build_your_own.title: Writing Your Own Toy Compiler Using Flex (C++)
build_your_own.description: Build a toy compiler in C++ using Flex and Bison.

build_your_own.title: Build Your Own Lisp
build_your_own.description: Build a Lisp interpreter in C while learning C itself. One of the best C learning projects available.

build_your_own.title: Crafting Interpreters
build_your_own.description: Build two complete interpreters — one in Java then one in C. The C version is the advanced, production-quality one.

build_your_own.title: A Regular Expression Matcher
build_your_own.description: Rob Pike's 30-line regex engine in C. One of the most beautiful pieces of code ever written. Translates directly to C++.

build_your_own.title: Learn How to Write a Hash Table in C
build_your_own.description: Step-by-step hash table implementation in C with open addressing and linear probing.

build_your_own.title: Writing a Linux Debugger
build_your_own.description: Build a gdb-like debugger in C++ using ptrace. Advanced but incredibly educational for understanding how programs execute.

build_your_own.title: Make Your Own Game Engine (video series)
build_your_own.description: YouTube series building a 2D game engine from scratch in C++.

build_your_own.title: How X Window Managers Work and How to Write One
build_your_own.description: Build a minimal X11 window manager in C++. Deep dive into how desktops actually work.

build_your_own.title: From NAND to Tetris
build_your_own.description: Build a complete computer from logic gates up through OS and compiler. Language-agnostic. One of the best CS projects ever designed.

build_your_own.title: Let's Make a Voxel Engine
build_your_own.description: Build a Minecraft-style voxel engine in C++. Covers chunk generation, rendering, and performance optimization.

build_your_own.title: Game Physics Series — Allen Chou
build_your_own.description: Multi-part series on game physics in C++. Covers rigid body dynamics, collision detection, and constraints.

build_your_own.title: Neural Networks: Zero to Hero — Karpathy
build_your_own.description: Andrej Karpathy's legendary neural network series. Python but the concepts translate to any language. Build backprop from scratch.

build_your_own.title: Write Yourself a Git
build_your_own.description: Implement Git from scratch in Python. Understand objects, refs, commits, and branches by building them.

build_your_own.title: Building a Container from Scratch in Go
build_your_own.description: Build Linux containers from scratch in Go. Understand namespaces, cgroups, and how Docker actually works.

build_your_own.title: Let's Build a Browser Engine
build_your_own.description: Build a toy browser layout engine in Rust. Covers HTML/CSS parsing and rendering.

build_your_own.title: Build Your Own Web Server From Scratch
build_your_own.description: Build an HTTP server from scratch in Node.js. Understand TCP, HTTP parsing, and request handling.

build_your_own.title: Learn Blockchains by Building One
build_your_own.description: Build a minimal blockchain in Python. Covers hashing, consensus, and chain validation.

build_your_own.title: The Super Tiny Compiler
build_your_own.description: A tiny compiler in ~200 lines. Teaches tokenizing, parsing, and code generation concepts clearly.

build_your_own.title: Introduction to Ray Tracing: a Simple Method for Creating 3D Images
build_your_own.description: Scratchapixel's introduction to ray tracing fundamentals. Builds intuition for 3D rendering math before diving into a full implementation.

build_your_own.title: How OpenGL Works: Software Rendering in 500 Lines of Code
build_your_own.description: Build a software renderer from scratch that mimics OpenGL. Covers rasterization, depth buffers, shaders, and texture mapping.

build_your_own.title: Rasterization: a Practical Implementation
build_your_own.description: Deep dive into rasterization — how GPUs turn triangles into pixels. The foundation of real-time 3D graphics.

build_your_own.title: Write Your Own Virtual Machine (LC-3)
build_your_own.description: Build an LC-3 virtual machine in C. Shows how CPUs actually work — registers, instruction sets, and memory-mapped I/O.

build_your_own.title: Writing a Game Boy Emulator, Cinoop
build_your_own.description: Step-by-step Game Boy emulator in C. Covers CPU emulation, memory, graphics, and input. A simpler entry point than a full NES emulator.

build_your_own.title: Handmade Hero
build_your_own.description: Casey Muratori builds a complete professional game from scratch in C — no libraries, no engine. Legendary for teaching how software really works.

build_your_own.title: How to Program an NES Game in C
build_your_own.description: Full guide to writing an NES game in C. Covers the NES hardware architecture, memory mapping, PPU, and sound.

build_your_own.title: Tetris Tutorial in C++ — Platform Independent
build_your_own.description: Build Tetris in C++ focused on game logic, not rendering. Platform-independent and well-paced for beginners.

build_your_own.title: The Little Book About OS Development
build_your_own.description: Short, practical guide to OS development. Covers bootloader, segmentation, paging, and a basic shell. Less intimidating than full OS courses.

build_your_own.title: Roll Your Own Toy UNIX-Clone OS
build_your_own.description: Classic OS tutorial building a UNIX-like OS from scratch with interrupts, paging, and a virtual filesystem.

build_your_own.title: Kernel 101 — Let's Write a Kernel
build_your_own.description: Minimal kernel in C that boots and prints to screen. Perfect first kernel — short and achievable in a weekend.

build_your_own.title: How to Create an OS from Scratch
build_your_own.description: 30 steps building your own OS starting from a 16-bit boot sector. Well-paced, from assembly boot to protected mode and C.

build_your_own.title: Learning OS Dev Using Linux Kernel and Raspberry Pi
build_your_own.description: Build a Linux-like OS for Raspberry Pi. ARM architecture, interrupts, and memory management on real hardware.

build_your_own.title: Writing an OS in Rust
build_your_own.description: Philipp Oppermann's OS series in Rust. Exceptionally well-written. Concepts (bare metal, interrupts, paging, heap) translate directly to C++.

build_your_own.title: Let's Build a Shell!
build_your_own.description: Shell workshop in C covering forking, exec, and pipes. Concise and focused. Good complement to the Brennan tutorial.

build_your_own.title: Writing a UNIX Shell
build_your_own.description: 3-part series on a UNIX shell from scratch. Covers the process lifecycle, signals, and job control in depth.

build_your_own.title: Baby's First Garbage Collector
build_your_own.description: Bob Nystrom's minimal mark-and-sweep GC in C. Short and clear — builds core intuition for memory management in language runtimes.

build_your_own.title: C Interpreter That Interprets Itself
build_your_own.description: A C interpreter in C that can run itself. Teaches recursive descent parsing, AST evaluation, and memory management from scratch.

build_your_own.title: The Super Tiny Compiler (Canonical)
build_your_own.description: The original ~200-line compiler. Best explanation of tokenizing, parsing, and code generation in existence. Read it, then rewrite it in C++.

build_your_own.title: How a 64k Intro Is Made
build_your_own.description: How to pack a real-time 3D demo into 64 kilobytes. Compression, procedural generation, shader tricks — extreme optimization.

build_your_own.title: How to Write a Video Player in Less Than 1000 Lines
build_your_own.description: Build a video player using FFmpeg in C. Learn media decoding, audio/video sync, and SDL rendering.

build_your_own.title: Sol — An MQTT Broker from Scratch
build_your_own.description: Build an MQTT message broker in C from scratch. Covers epoll, event loops, and the MQTT binary protocol.

build_your_own.title: Write a System Call
build_your_own.description: Add a custom system call to the Linux kernel. Shows exactly how user space crosses into kernel space.

build_your_own.title: The Very Basics of a Terminal Emulator
build_your_own.description: Build a minimal terminal emulator. Pseudoterminals, escape codes, and X11 interaction — the plumbing behind every terminal you use.

build_your_own.title: Building a BitTorrent Client from the Ground Up in Go
build_your_own.description: Full BitTorrent client in Go. Bencode parsing, peer discovery, piece downloading. Protocol engineering at its best. Translates well to C++.

build_your_own.title: Building Blockchain in Go
build_your_own.description: 6-part series building a Bitcoin-like blockchain in Go. Hashing, proof of work, transactions, wallets, and networking.

build_your_own.title: Linux Containers in 500 Lines of Code
build_your_own.description: Minimal Linux container implementation in C. Shows how Docker actually works — namespaces, cgroups, chroot, capabilities.

build_your_own.title: Build Your Own Regular Expression Engines
build_your_own.description: Build backtracking, NFA, and DFA regex engines from scratch. Three approaches to the same problem. Translates cleanly to C++.

build_your_own.title: Building a DNS Server in Rust
build_your_own.description: Build a full DNS resolver from scratch. Binary protocol parsing, UDP, and recursive resolution. Directly translatable to C++.

build_your_own.title: Let's Build A Web Server
build_your_own.description: 3-part series on an HTTP server from scratch. Covers sockets, HTTP parsing, and WSGI. Core networking concepts that translate to C++.

build_your_own.title: A Neural Network in 11 Lines of Python
build_your_own.description: The simplest possible neural network from scratch in pure Python. Build core intuition before tackling Karpathy's deeper series.

build_your_own.title: Build Your Own React
build_your_own.description: Rebuild React from scratch step by step — virtual DOM, reconciliation, hooks. Teaches tree diffing algorithms valuable in any language.

build_your_own.title: Building a Vector Space Search Engine in Python
build_your_own.description: Build a TF-IDF search engine from scratch. Tokenization, indexing, and ranking — core information retrieval concepts.

build_your_own.title: Build a Large Language Model From Scratch
build_your_own.description: Sebastian Raschka's LLM from scratch in Python. Transformers, attention, pretraining — the foundations of modern AI.

build_your_own.title: Build Your Own Database from Scratch (B+Tree to SQL)
build_your_own.description: Build a relational database from B+trees up to SQL in 3000 lines of Go. The most complete from-scratch DB tutorial available.

build_your_own.title: From Source Code To Machine Code: Build Your Own Compiler
build_your_own.description: Build a compiler from scratch in Python — lexer, parser, code gen. Practical, modern approach to compiler construction.

build_your_own.title: Building a Software and Hardware Stack for a Simple Computer
build_your_own.description: Video series building a complete computer system from scratch — hardware, OS, compiler, and apps. The full stack.

projects.title: Try-To-Orbit
projects.description: Physics-based orbital simulation game in C++. Starts as a CLI physics console and evolves into a gravitational mechanics simulator. Flagship learning project.

projects.title: Bugg Bot — Discord Bot
projects.description: Multi-purpose Discord bot in Python with weather, games, Pokemon battles, and productivity tools. 68 commands across 10 cogs.

projects.title: Personal Portfolio (ASCII River)
projects.description: Interactive ASCII river scene portfolio. Fisherman catches sections of the portfolio. Built with vanilla HTML/CSS/JS.

projects.title: The CPP Grind
projects.description: This site. C++ learning platform with articles, projects, daily games, and curated resources. Built while learning C++.

projects.title: Mini-CLI-Calc
projects.description: Minimal command-line calculator in C++ with REPL mode, operator dispatch, and input validation.

projects.title: Binary Inspector
projects.description: Displays binary/hex representations and IEEE-754 floating-point decomposition for any numeric value.

projects.title: Fork Tree Visualizer
projects.description: Traces the process tree produced by fork() calls, printing each process with PID, PPID, and which fork created it.

projects.title: Thread Memory Lab
projects.description: Shows how global, static, local, and heap variables behave across process and thread boundaries.

projects.title: Process Pipeline
projects.description: Three-stage concurrent pipeline using POSIX message queues: sender, processor, and logger via fork().

projects.title: Maze Solver
projects.description: Recursive backtracking maze solver. Reads 2D text maze, marks solution path, reports steps and cells visited.

projects.title: Sorting Visualizer
projects.description: Quicksort and mergesort side by side with timing, comparison counts, and step-by-step visualization.

projects.title: Network Analyzer
projects.description: C++ port of Java graph analyzer. Dijkstra's, articulation points, BFS connected components.

projects.title: Float Inspector
projects.description: CLI IEEE-754 bit inspector showing sign, exponent, mantissa, GRS bits, and rounding decisions.

projects.title: COD 5e Solutions
projects.description: Solutions for Patterson & Hennessy Computer Organization and Design 5th Edition.
